summary: During prenatal development, the ethmoidal sinuses are one of the earliest nasal cavity structures to appear; they initiate formation prior to or around birth compared with other paranasal sinuses like maxillary, frontal, sphenoid which follow later stages post-birth. Thus, for this context, we conclude that the ethmoidals represent the initial set of developed paranasal sinuses upon birth. Answer: \boxed{\text{Ethmoid}} Agent2’s reasoning focuses on understanding normal anatomical and physiological developments associated with infant growth where it identifies the ethmoidal sinuses as being amongst the very first formed due their initiation even within utero. This aligns directly answering what specific type develops initially after birth from provided options while emphasizing timing relative to others mentioned such as maxillary etc., all part of broader
